name: True Parallax
description: A reworked, more accurate parallax effect for Rain World that works by warping the entire screen according to level geometry, making the game look slightly zoomed-in and 3-dimensional. This effect is like an advanced form of Parallax Occlusion Mapping.<LINE><LINE>In order to support a variety of devices and player preferences, this mod has been made highly configurable. Please check out the remix/config menu for this mod and browse through the handful of available presets. Or if you're feeling adventurous, try fiddling with some of the settings and make your own preset!<LINE><LINE>Mod compatibility:<LINE>* SBCameraScroll: This mod has been specifically designed for compatibility with SBCameraScroll. If you discover any issues with combining the two mods, treat them as bugs and let me know.<LINE>* Sharpener: Sharpener causes absolutely no issues. And because this mod zooms in the camera slightly, I recommend using Sharpener!<LINE>* Stick Together Co-op + SBCameraScroll: The dynamic zoom-out feature of Stick Together Co-op does technically work with this mod, but it just looks terrible. I do not recommend using this mod alongside Stick Together Co-op.<LINE>* Split-screen Co-op: For the most part, NOT compatible. The parallax works for the first camera only, and it is automatically disabled for any other cameras. So having this mod active hopefully shouldn't cause any issues, because its designed to disable itself for split-screen.<LINE><LINE>How this differs from my previous parallax mod:<LINE>* The previous mod only warped the room texture, whereas this mod warps the entire screen AFTER everything is rendered (except the HUD). Although warping the room texture had some minor advantages, warping the entire screen improves visual consistency (especially lighting) and allows for a superior 3D effect. Warping just the room texture restricted me to only implementing a sort of "reverse parallax" effect, whereas having control over the entire screen removes that restriction. Setting the Pivot Depth to 0.17 replicates the old "reverse parallax" effect (not recommended).<LINE>* This mod has been completely rewritten from scratch to be much more robust and efficient than the previous mod. It's basically just better in almost every way.<LINE><LINE>Known shortcomings of this mod:<LINE>1. Pixels are different around creatures and items, creating an undesirable sort of blur effect. This is an essential limitation of this mod: The shader does not know what is "behind" creatures, so it has to "make it up."<LINE>2. When the screen moves, pixel lighting and decals can be misaligned, sometimes causing unpleasant flickering. In vanilla, this happens only briefly during screen transitions, but the effect is much more noticeable when using SBCameraScroll. This is a vanilla bug; it is merely far more noticeable when using this mod (because it zooms in and expects pixel-perfect alignment).<LINE>3. Some objects, especially translucent ones such as holograms, are drawn on the back wall. This is due to the game rendering them differently than it renders creatures/items (which are opaque). There is no easy way to fix this.<LINE>4. Lag. Sorry, I tried to minimize it.<LINE><LINE>How you can help:<LINE>1. Report bugs you find. Please mention which room it occurred in and any potentially-relevant mods you had active. I can't promise I'll fix every bug, but I still appreciate knowing about them.<LINE>2. Give feedback about the mod. A quick comment from you can really help me make this mod better.<LINE>3. Experiment with the mod config settings and share any presets you make. Presets are saved as .TXT files, so you can easily copy them and share them here on Steam (I'll pin a discussion) or DM TheLazyCowboy1 on Discord. I might add your preset to the mod for everyone to use!<LINE>4. If you can code and this mod ever gets broken by an update (or if you just want to improve anything about it), please feel free to upload your own version of this mod. I might not have much free time in the future to maintain my mods, but they're all open source if anyone wants to take them.
